title: Product Manager, YouTube Create
role_summary: PM for YouTube Create — a consumer creator tool that helps YouTubers produce content. This is a core consumer product role at YouTube focused on creation tools.
why_good_fit: Jay's creator economy experience is directly transferable: he scaled Resso's creator-facing features, led TikTok SoundOn music distribution, built the Ghost Music Tab for artist activation, and worked on creator monetisation research at ByteDance. At Meta, he contributed to content relevance and creator growth for Indian markets, and was a core member of the Shoppertainment initiative focused on creator-commerce convergence. His deep understanding of regional/long-tail Indian creators and GenZ content consumption patterns (GenZ Product Council) makes him uniquely positioned. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7 years, perfect seniority match.
how_to_win: Lead with the Resso founding story — scaling a creator-centric music product from alpha to India's largest streaming app. Emphasise the 10%/90% content insight that shaped recommendation strategy. Then bridge to Meta's creator growth work and Shoppertainment. Frame yourself as someone who understands the full creator journey — from creation tools to distribution to monetisation — across ByteDance and Meta. Highlight your field-level understanding of how Indian creators (especially Tier 2/3, regional language) actually create content, and how that differs from global assumptions.
job_description: You'll own product strategy and execution for YouTube Create, a suite of tools that help creators produce and edit content directly within the YouTube ecosystem. Day-to-day involves defining the product roadmap, running user research with creators, working with engineering and design to ship features, and measuring impact on creator output and engagement. Success means more creators using YouTube's native tools to produce higher-quality content, driving creator retention and platform growth.

title: Product Manager, YouTube Live Living Room
role_summary: PM for YouTube's live streaming experience on TV/living room devices. Consumer video product at the intersection of live content and the big screen.
why_good_fit: Jay has direct live streaming and video product experience from ByteDance (Resso, TikTok) and Meta (content relevance, Shoppertainment which involves live commerce). His understanding of Indian consumer video consumption — especially how Tier 2/3 users engage with live content — is a differentiator. His GenZ Product Council membership gives him insight into evolving content consumption patterns. Only 3 years PM experience required; Jay exceeds this comfortably with 7 years, making him a strong senior candidate. His Shoppertainment work at Meta directly involved live streaming as a commerce surface.
how_to_win: Pitch the Shoppertainment story — how live streaming is becoming a commerce and engagement surface in India, and how you identified this as a monetisation big bet at Meta. Connect to your understanding of Indian living room behaviour (shared devices, family viewing, regional content preferences). Reference your Resso experience with content discovery and recommendation for diverse Indian audiences. Show you understand the unique UX challenges of TV interfaces vs. mobile. Frame yourself as someone who can bring India market intelligence to a global product surface.
job_description: You'll own the YouTube Live experience on TV and living room devices — think smart TVs, streaming sticks, and gaming consoles. Day-to-day involves defining features that make live content more engaging on big screens, working with engineering to ship, running experiments, and analysing viewer behaviour. Success looks like increased live viewership on TV devices, higher engagement during live streams, and a differentiated living room experience that drives YouTube TV usage.

title: Product Manager, Google Pay
role_summary: PM for Google Pay — India's leading digital payments consumer product. Core consumer fintech role with massive India market relevance.
why_good_fit: Google Pay is one of the most important India consumer products at Google, and Jay's superpower is translating Indian user behaviour into product decisions. His SMB work at Meta (bakery insight → 2.3% revenue uplift, 10M businesses impacted) directly maps to Google Pay's merchant/SMB ecosystem. His understanding of informal Indian businesses, voice-led WhatsApp operations, and Tier 2/3 digital adoption patterns is exactly what Google Pay needs to grow beyond metro users. His Shoppertainment and commerce work adds a payments-adjacent lens. 5 years experience required — Jay has 7.
how_to_win: Lead with the Dahod bakery story — it's a perfect Google Pay narrative about understanding how real Indian SMBs transact. Emphasise that you uncovered how enforcement models built on global assumptions failed Indian SMBs, and you drove a 2.3% revenue uplift by fixing this. Draw the parallel: Google Pay faces similar challenges in understanding how Tier 2/3 merchants and consumers actually use digital payments vs. how product teams assume they do. Highlight your field research methodology — going to users, not waiting for data. Show your understanding of India's UPI ecosystem and informal commerce patterns.
job_description: You'll own a product area within Google Pay, India's largest digital payments platform. Day-to-day involves defining product strategy for consumer or merchant-facing features, running user research, working with engineering and design to ship, and measuring impact on transaction volume and user growth. Success means driving adoption of Google Pay features among Indian consumers and merchants, particularly in underserved segments.

title: Product Manager, eWallets
role_summary: PM for Google's eWallet products — digital wallet infrastructure likely serving Google Pay and other payment surfaces in India and emerging markets.
why_good_fit: Jay's deep India market intelligence and SMB understanding directly applies to eWallet product development. His work on SMB monetisation at Meta (understanding how informal businesses handle money, the bakery insight, 10M businesses impacted) gives him a unique lens on digital wallet adoption challenges in India. His experience with Tier 2/3 users and understanding of digital literacy gaps is critical for eWallet products targeting mass-market India. 5 years experience required — Jay has 7.
how_to_win: Frame your SMB enforcement work as fundamentally about understanding how Indian businesses handle money and digital tools — the same core challenge eWallets face. Highlight your field research methodology: you don't rely on dashboards alone, you go to Dahod and talk to bakery owners. Show how your understanding of voice-led, informal digital commerce in India can inform eWallet product design. Reference your Shoppertainment work as evidence you understand the commerce-payments intersection.
job_description: You'll own product strategy for Google's eWallet products, likely focused on stored value, wallet infrastructure, and payment flows. Day-to-day involves defining features, working with engineering and compliance teams, running user research on wallet adoption patterns, and measuring transaction metrics. Success means growing wallet adoption and transaction volume, particularly in emerging markets like India.

title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
role_summary: PM for payments risk — managing fraud detection, risk models, and trust/safety for Google's payment products. Consumer-adjacent fintech role.
why_good_fit: Jay's SMB over-enforcement work at Meta is an almost perfect parallel — he identified that global enforcement/risk models were producing false positives that devastated Indian SMBs, and he drove the creation of an agentic resolution system. This is exactly the kind of thinking a Payments Risk PM needs: balancing fraud prevention with user experience, understanding that risk models built on global assumptions can harm legitimate Indian users. His policy change advocacy and cross-functional work with enforcement teams maps directly. 3 years experience required — Jay has 7, making him a strong senior candidate.
how_to_win: The SMB over-enforcement story IS this job. Lead with it: 'At Meta, I discovered that enforcement models built on global assumptions were blocking 10M+ legitimate Indian businesses. I drove a policy change and the creation of an agentic resolution system, resulting in a 2.3% India revenue uplift.' Frame this as risk management expertise — you understand the tension between aggressive fraud prevention and false positives, especially in India's unique digital commerce landscape. Show how your field research methodology can uncover risk model blind spots that data alone can't reveal.
job_description: You'll own the risk management product surface for Google's payment products — defining fraud detection strategies, building risk models, and ensuring legitimate users aren't blocked while bad actors are caught. Day-to-day involves working with data science, engineering, and policy teams to balance risk tolerance with user experience. Success means reducing fraud losses while minimising false positives and maintaining user trust.

title: Product Manager, Payments Platform
role_summary: PM for Google's payments platform infrastructure — the underlying systems powering Google Pay and other payment products.
why_good_fit: Jay's SMB and commerce experience at Meta (2.3% revenue uplift, 10M businesses, Shoppertainment) gives him a strong understanding of payment ecosystems from the user/merchant side. His India market intelligence is valuable for a payments platform serving Indian users. However, this is more platform/infrastructure-oriented than consumer-facing, which is a slight mismatch with Jay's strongest skills. 5 years experience required — Jay has 7.
how_to_win: Position yourself as someone who understands the end-user impact of platform decisions. Use the SMB enforcement story to show how platform-level decisions (enforcement models, resolution systems) directly impact millions of businesses and revenue. Emphasise your ability to bridge the gap between technical platform teams and on-ground user reality. Show that you can bring the 'why does this matter for the Indian merchant?' perspective to platform product decisions.
job_description: You'll own a part of Google's payments platform — the infrastructure layer that powers payment processing, settlement, and merchant services. Day-to-day involves defining platform capabilities, working with engineering on reliability and scalability, and ensuring the platform serves the needs of consumer-facing payment products. Success means a robust, scalable payments platform that enables product teams to ship faster.

title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps
role_summary: PM for Google Play's apps ecosystem — the world's largest app distribution platform. Consumer platform role with massive India relevance.
why_good_fit: Google Play is the primary app distribution channel for India's 500M+ Android users, and Jay's deep understanding of Indian consumer behaviour across Tier 2/3 segments is directly relevant. His experience scaling Resso (a Play Store app) from alpha to India's largest music streaming app gives him firsthand understanding of the developer/publisher side. His work on content discovery (Resso's Explore page curation, the 10%/90% insight) maps to app discovery challenges on Play. 5 years experience required — Jay has 7.
how_to_win: Lead with the Resso story — you literally built and scaled an app on Google Play from zero to India's largest music streaming platform. You understand the developer journey, the discovery challenge, and what drives app adoption in India. Highlight your content curation and recommendation experience (Explore page at Resso) as directly applicable to app discovery. Show your understanding of Indian app consumption patterns — regional apps, gaming, fintech — and how Play can better serve Tier 2/3 users.
job_description: You'll own a product area within Google Play's apps ecosystem — potentially app discovery, developer tools, or user engagement features. Day-to-day involves defining product strategy, running experiments, working with engineering and design, and measuring impact on app installs, developer satisfaction, and user engagement. Success means growing the Play Store ecosystem in key markets like India.

title: Product Manager, AI Gaming, Google Play
role_summary: PM for AI-powered gaming features on Google Play — intersection of AI product development and gaming ecosystem on the world's largest app store.
why_good_fit: This role sits at the intersection of AI product (Jay's top priority) and consumer platform (Google Play). Jay's AI fluency (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS, 20+ AI user interviews) combined with his app ecosystem experience (Resso on Play Store) makes this a compelling fit. His understanding of Indian gaming consumption patterns and Tier 2/3 user behaviour adds market intelligence value. However, gaming-specific experience is limited. 5 years experience required — Jay has 7.
how_to_win: Lead with your AI fluency story — top 5% Metamate user, built a personal AI operating system, conducted 20+ AI user interviews discovering that language/tone matters more than factual correctness for Indian GenZ. Then bridge to your Play Store experience via Resso. Frame yourself as someone who can bring AI product intuition AND India market intelligence to the gaming vertical. Acknowledge the gaming domain gap but position your user research methodology as the way you'd rapidly build domain expertise.
job_description: You'll own AI-powered features for the gaming vertical on Google Play — potentially AI-driven game discovery, developer tools using AI, or AI-enhanced gaming experiences. Day-to-day involves defining AI product strategy for gaming, working with ML engineers and game developers, running experiments, and measuring impact on gaming engagement and developer adoption. Success means AI features that meaningfully improve the gaming experience on Play.

title: Product Manager, Applied AI, Google Cloud
role_summary: PM for Applied AI products on Google Cloud — bringing GenAI/LLM capabilities to enterprise customers. AI-native role with B2B focus.

His experience with enterprise/SMB segments at Meta adds B2B context. Requires GenAI/LLM domain experience, which Jay has from the user/product side. However, this is B2B Cloud, not consumer — a moderate mismatch. 5 years experience required — Jay has 7.
how_to_win: Lead with your AI operating system story — you built a multi-agent system inside Metamate that automates insight writing, competitive intelligence, stakeholder engagement, and more. You're not just an AI user; you're an AI power builder. Then share the MetaAI India field work insights — language as a product growth lever, voice-led AI opportunities. Day-to-day involves defining AI product strategy, working with ML engineers and cloud sales teams, running customer discovery, and measuring adoption metrics. Success means enterprise customers successfully deploying Google's AI capabilities in production.

It's a deeply technical infrastructure product with no consumer, India market, or AI growth component. While it's at Google (target company), the domain mismatch is significant.
how_to_win: This would require Jay to heavily lean on his data analysis skills and reframe his analytics dashboard work (Unidash at Meta) as database-adjacent. Not recommended as a priority application given the domain gap.
job_description: You'll own a Google Cloud database product (e.g., Cloud SQL, Spanner, Bigtable), defining features for enterprise customers who need managed database services. Success means growing database product adoption and revenue on Google Cloud.
